name: lambda-optimization-advisor
description: Reviews AWS Lambda functions for performance, memory configuration, and cost optimization. Activates when users write Lambda handlers or discuss Lambda performance.
capabilities: Detects sequential async operations that can be parallelized with tokio::try_join
Identifies resource initialization patterns that create new clients on every invocation
Reviews Cargo.toml configurations for release profile optimizations
Recommends ARM64 architecture for 20% better price-performance
Analyzes memory configuration to optimize CPU allocation and execution time
Suggests batch processing patterns to reduce invocation overhead
author: EmilLindfors
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 25 potential issues in SKILL.md code examples, all of which are false positives. The flagged patterns (external_commands, network URLs, cryptographic references) are markdown documentation examples illustrating Lambda optimization patterns, not executable code. The skill only uses read-only tools (Read, Grep, Glob) for code analysis. No malicious intent or dangerous patterns present.
valueStatement: AWS Lambda functions often suffer from slow cold starts and high costs due to inefficient patterns. The Lambda Optimization Advisor analyzes Lambda code and suggests specific improvements for performance, memory configuration, and cost reduction.
limitations: Only supports Rust-based Lambda functions using lambda_runtime
Cannot modify code directly - only provides suggestions
Does not analyze deployed Lambda configurations or permissions
Cannot test optimizations in a live environment

useCases.title: New Lambda Project Setup
useCases.description: Review a new Rust Lambda handler during development to establish optimal patterns from the start, avoiding costly refactoring later.

useCases.title: Performance Troubleshooting
useCases.description: Diagnose why an existing Lambda function has high latency or cold start issues and receive specific code changes to improve performance.

useCases.title: Cost Optimization Review
useCases.description: Analyze Lambda functions to identify opportunities for reducing execution costs through better memory configuration and concurrent operations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Lambda Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this AWS Lambda handler code for performance and cost optimizations. Identify any sequential async operations, resource initialization issues, or configuration problems.

promptTemplates.title: Cold Start Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this Lambda function to find causes of slow cold starts. Look for patterns that create new clients or resources on each invocation and suggest fixes using OnceLock or static initialization.

promptTemplates.title: Cargo.toml Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this Lambda project's Cargo.toml file. Check if the release profile is optimized for Lambda deployment with settings like opt-level, lto, codegen-units, and strip for smaller binaries.

promptTemplates.title: ARM64 Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: This Lambda function is running on x86_64. Evaluate whether migrating to ARM64 (Graviton2) would provide better price-performance and what code or build changes would be needed.

bestPractices: Use tokio::try_join! or tokio::join! for concurrent I/O operations instead of sequential await statements
Initialize HTTP clients, database pools, and AWS SDK clients using OnceLock or lazy_static for container reuse
Configure Cargo.toml release profile with opt-level = 'z', lto = true, codegen-units = 1, and strip = true
antiPatterns: Creating new clients or resources inside the Lambda handler function (happens on every invocation)
Making sequential async calls that could run concurrently (wastes execution time)
Using default Cargo release profile without optimization flags (larger binaries, slower cold starts)

faq.question: Does this skill modify my Lambda code?
faq.answer: No, this skill only analyzes your code and provides optimization suggestions with example code changes. You must apply the changes yourself.

faq.question: Which Lambda runtimes are supported?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Rust Lambda functions using the lambda_runtime crate. Other runtimes like Python or Node.js are not within scope.

faq.question: How much performance improvement can I expect?
faq.answer: Results vary based on your current patterns. Converting sequential I/O to concurrent operations typically provides 3-5x speedup. ARM64 migration offers 20% better price-performance on average.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Lambda permissions or IAM roles?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on code-level performance optimizations. It does not analyze IAM policies, resource-based permissions, or VPC configurations.

faq.question: Should I always use ARM64 for Lambda?
faq.answer: ARM64 provides better price-performance for most workloads, but some legacy x86-only libraries or dependencies may require x86_64 architecture. Test your specific workload before migrating.

faq.question: How do I test the suggested optimizations?
faq.answer: Apply the suggested changes to your code, run your existing tests, and deploy to a test environment. Use Lambda Power Tuning to compare performance before and after changes.
